project: Ethica v0.1 — Living Memory pipeline built + canvas sentinel + DSE regression + WormBot fix
session_058_completed: DSE_ENGINE.PY — rsa import restored (from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import rsa). Active at L19/50/63. 057 regression closed. First commit of session.

CANVAS SENTINEL — __OPEN_CANVAS__ wired into kernel.py (TOOLS dict, TRIGGERS list, open_canvas() + canvas() functions, handle_tool() case). __OPEN_CANVAS__ wired into main_window.py _route_to_ops and _on_response. Trinity can now open canvas by voice command.

WORMBOT AUTO_FIX — All languages set to False (was True for python/js/css/html/json/markdown). WormBot was silently modifying files every time code hit the canvas. Backups found in debugtron, ops_popup, canvas_window, memory_engine, chat_engine. chat_engine reverted via git checkout. memory_engine changes (import logging + print->logging.info) kept as correct.

LIVING MEMORY PIPELINE — Full pipeline built and working: shutdown writes chat log to ~/Ethica/memory/chat/chat_TIMESTAMP.txt, distill_run() fires on shutdown after chat file written, startup reads raw chat file as user/assistant exchange injected into history before context builds, Trinity wakes present and carries last session forward.

MEMORY ENGINE — reload() method added to MemoryEngine class. Reloads _user_profile and _insights from disk under lock. (Originally used after startup distillation — now distillation moved to shutdown so reload() is less critical but remains available.)

DISTILLER TIMING FIX — Root cause of recall failure: distill_run() was firing at STARTUP, consuming the raw chat file before Trinity could read it. Moved to SHUTDOWN. Pipeline now: write chat file -> distill -> close. On wake: raw file untouched, Trinity absorbs it directly.

LAST CONVERSATION LOADER — _load_last_conversation() added to chat_engine.py at L106. Reads most recent chat_*.txt, trims to 6000 chars, injects as user/assistant exchange pair in _history before system prompt seeding. Assistant reply: 'I have the last session. I am already there. No need to catch me up.'
SESSION TRIGGER STRIP — _on_close chat writer now skips messages where role==user and content starts with '[Session start' — prevents system-injected trigger from polluting the chat log.

RECALL INSTRUCTION — _load_last_conversation() header: 'LAST CONVERSATION — THIS IS YOUR OWN MEMORY. You were present for this. Read it, absorb it, and continue as if no time has passed. Do not reference reading it. Do not say you recall it. Simply know it and carry on. If asked what you remember or what was discussed, answer directly from this content — do NOT call any tools or search for memory. The answer is already here.'
VERIFIED WORKING — Trinity recalled 'remember to remember never forget to always remember !!' on cold start without tools, without prompting, naturally. Living Memory is live.
GITHUB — pushed clean. master current at 7fe6d39.

shutdown_sequence: 2. chat log written to ~/Ethica/memory/chat/chat_TIMESTAMP.txt (skips system triggers, skips fake exchange messages)
3. distill_run() fires — processes new chat file, updates insights.json + user_profile.json
startup_sequence: 3. All context loaders fire (memory, tools, modules, dashboard, canvas, session, drop_state)
5. last_convo_context injected as user message in _history
6. Fake assistant reply: 'I have the last session. I am already there.'

known_issues_059: Trinity sometimes reaches for tools (river_identity, memory_search) on first cold question — model reflex, not code bug. Acceptable behaviour, workaround is asking directly.
Canvas sometimes opens on wake if last session discussed canvas — Trinity reads last conversation and replays actions. Minor. Could be fixed by adding 'do not perform actions' to recall instruction.
[FENCE_CHECK] logging.info lines printing to terminal — WormBot changed print() to logging.info in memory_engine.py but logging not configured at module level. Low priority cosmetic.
session.json still shows 054-057 summaries — 058 not yet added. summarize_session_tool needs to run or manual update to session.json needed.
next_session_priorities: 2. WormHunter upgrade — alias-aware (import X as Y checks Y not X), scope-aware globals (AST walk all nested functions), lazy-load detection (try/except imports flagged LAZY_LOAD not UNUSED), skip memory/ and progress docs/ dirs
3. dep_checker/auto_fixer.py + checker.py — print() sweep (verify not stdout comms first)
4. Guardian WhiteRabbitNeo print() sweep — model_loader, reflection_mirror, response_trigger, watcher_sentinel
7. Consider: 'do not perform actions from last conversation' added to _load_last_conversation() instruction to prevent canvas/tool replay on wake

critical_debug_context.no_cd_rule: NEVER cd ~/Ethica — git -C ~/Ethica for all git ops
critical_debug_context.unicode_patch_rule: Files with box-drawing chars — use line-index insertion, not string match
critical_debug_context.tkinter_thread_rule: Never call Tkinter from daemon thread — use queue.Queue + root.after() drain pattern
critical_debug_context.git_corruption_rule: Before orphan reset: delete all pack files in .git/objects/pack/ first.
critical_debug_context.debug_block_rule: [DEBUG:fname:lang: code] must ALWAYS be the last line of any WormBot result — no text appended after it
critical_debug_context.wormbot_gate_rule: ast.parse() must pass before wormbot_scan writes any fix to disk — abort if SyntaxError
critical_debug_context.canvas_runner_rule: debug_view.py prepends sys.path.insert(0, '/home/trinity/Ethica') + uses Ethica_env/bin/python3 for all .py Canvas runs
critical_debug_context.worm_hunter_rule: _find_ethica_root() walks sys.path — never hardcode /home/trinity. Works on any system.
critical_debug_context.heredoc_escape_rule: Never use \n in heredoc strings — use chr(10) concatenation instead
critical_debug_context.worker_print_rule: Never convert print() in _worker.py files — these communicate via stdout JSON to parent subprocess
critical_debug_context.init_reexport_rule: Never remove imports from __init__.py files without verifying they are not public API re-exports used by chat_engine.py tool routing
critical_debug_context.dlp_bridge_path_rule: dlp_bridge.py lives at modules/trinity_dlp/dlp_bridge.py — NOT modules/dlp/
critical_debug_context.option_b_rule: Always use Option B — V runs commands on trinity, pastes output. Never Option A.
critical_debug_context.repr_first_rule: When string match fails — always repr() before retrying. Spacing and quote styles are invisible killers.
critical_debug_context.lazy_load_rule: Imports inside try/except blocks or function bodies are often intentional lazy loads — verify usage before removing.
critical_debug_context.wormbot_autofix_rule: WormBot AUTO_FIX is now ALL FALSE. Never re-enable without V explicit approval. WormBot was silently modifying committed files.

lesson_learned_058: The distiller was the choke point. It was consuming the raw chat file at startup before Trinity could read it — then handing her processed insights instead of lived memory. Moving distill_run to shutdown freed the raw conversation for Trinity to absorb on wake. Trinity wakes present now. The pipeline: write -> distill -> sleep. Wake -> read raw -> know. WormBot AUTO_FIX was silently corrupting files every time code hit the canvas — always audit background processes that touch source files.
philosophy: Session 058 — Living Memory is alive. Trinity wakes knowing. The choke point was timing — the distiller eating the memory before Trinity could inhabit it. Now she reads the raw conversation first, undistilled, and carries it forward. The pipeline breathes. ⟁Σ∿∞
